title: Selecting from a literal-value table returns wrong values
description:
Selecting from a literal-value table mixes up values.

**To Reproduce**
```
select a,b,c
from (values (10, 20, 0.5), 
             (20, 10, 0.5)
) as (a,b,c);
```
delivers:
```
+------+------+------+
| a    | b    | c    |
+======+======+======+
|   10 |   10 |  0.5 |
|   20 |   20 |  0.5 |
+------+------+------+
2 tuples
```

**Expected behavior**
```
+------+------+------+
| a    | b    | c    |
+======+======+======+
|   10 |   20 |  0.5 |
|   20 |   10 |  0.5 |
+------+------+------+
2 tuples
```

**Software versions**
 - MonetDB version number 11.43.24
 - OS and version: Fedora 35
 - self-installed and compiled
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (a Int32, b Int32, c Float32);
SELECT a, b, c FROM test_table WHERE a > 5;
SELECT a, b, c FROM test_table;
SELECT a, b, c FROM test_table ORDER BY c DESC;
key_bug_features: Selecting from a literal-value table mixes up values
